_comment: A2A cross-bridge handoff config. Copy to handoff.local.json in your BRIDGE_HOME ($HOME/.agent-bridge by default), edit, then `chmod 0600` it. This file is data-only JSON — it is parsed, never executed. It carries peer-pair HMAC secrets, so it MUST be mode 0600 and MUST stay git-ignored. See docs/a2a-cross-bridge.md.
_bridge_id_comment: REQUIRED. This bridge's own id. It is the authenticated sender identity: the delivery runner signs it + sends it as the X-AGB-Peer header, and a peer receiver looks it up in its inbound peer allowlist. A receiving peer's config must list a peer entry whose 'id' equals this value.

listen._comment: The receiver binds to this tailnet IP ONLY. Leave 'address' empty to auto-select the first `tailscale ip`. The daemon FAILS CLOSED if the address is a wildcard (0.0.0.0/::), loopback, or cannot be proven to be in this node's actual Tailscale address set (the exact output of `tailscale ip`) — there is no CIDR-shape fallback, and if the tailscale CLI is unavailable the daemon refuses to serve.
listen._identity_comment: OPTIONAL (P0 self-heal): instead of (or in addition to) a raw 'address', you may key this node on a Tailscale IDENTITY that is resolved to the current IP at bind time via `tailscale status --json` — so an IP change after a tailnet re-login is picked up transparently. Precedence: 'node_id' (Tailscale StableID) wins over 'tailscale_name' (MagicDNS hostname or short HostName) wins over the legacy 'address'. When an identity is set, the resolved candidate STILL must pass the unchanged fail-closed bind proof (it must be in this node's `tailscale ip` set) — resolution only produces the candidate, it never weakens the proof. Examples (uncomment one): "node_id": "nDEADBEEF", "tailscale_name": "my-host" (or "my-host.your-tailnet.ts.net").

peers._comment: Each peer is an ordered-pair relationship. 'address' is the peer's tailnet IP — the receiver enforces remote_addr == this value. 'secret' is the shared HMAC key for this pair; 'secret_next' is optional and only set during a rotation overlap window. 'inbound_allowlist' lists the LOCAL agents this peer may enqueue to (exact match, no wildcard).
peers._identity_comment: OPTIONAL (P0 self-heal): a peer may carry a Tailscale IDENTITY resolved to the peer's current IP at send-time via `tailscale status --json`, killing the stale-IP class (an IP change after the peer re-logs-in is picked up on the next send — no manual handoff.local.json edit). Precedence: 'node_id' (the peer's Tailscale StableID) wins over 'tailscale_name' (its MagicDNS hostname / short HostName) wins over the legacy 'address'. If an identity is set but cannot be resolved, the sender HARD-ERRORS (delivery retries; it never silently sends to a possibly-stale 'address'). Examples (uncomment one): "node_id": "nCAFED00D", "tailscale_name": "bridge-b-host" (or "bridge-b-host.your-tailnet.ts.net").
